name: Central African Republic

colors.symbolism: the blood of humanity, common to all peoples; the shared blood spilled for independence

description: Four equal horizontal stripes — blue, white, green, yellow from top to bottom — overlaid by a single vertical red stripe down the centre, dividing the horizontal stripes in half. A yellow five-pointed star is placed in the upper-hoist quadrant, on the blue stripe.
history: Designed by Barthélemy Boganda, the founder of the Republic, and adopted on 1 December 1958 when the territory of Ubangi-Shari became autonomous within the French Community. It was retained when the country gained full independence on 13 August 1960 and has remained the national flag since. Boganda intended the combination of French tricolour and Pan-African colours to symbolise the union of France and Africa.

features.symbolism: four horizontal stripes (blue, white, green, yellow) crossed by a single vertical red stripe, fusing the French tricolour (blue, white, red) with the Pan-African colours (red, green, yellow) to symbolise unity between France and Africa and between Europe and Africa
the guiding star toward independence and progress; sometimes interpreted as showing the path forward for the country
